stem:
The table shows the distribution of preferred drinks among 100 employees at three branches of a company.

DrinkBranch ABranch BBranch CTotal
Coffee108725
Tea56920
Water15161445
Juice05510
Total303535100

If one of these employees is selected at random, what is the probability that the employee prefers tea?
rationale:
Choice A is correct. There are 20 tea drinkers out of 100 employees, so the probability is 20/100 = 1/5.

Choice B is incorrect; this is the probability of preferring coffee. Choice C is incorrect; this is the probability of preferring water. Choice D is incorrect; this is the probability of preferring juice.
